verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-resident HSP70 chaperone retained by the KDEL motif. However, it is well-documented to traffic to the cell surface under stress conditions (ER stress, hypoxia, oncogenic transformation, oxidative stress) where it appears on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Cell-surface GRP78 (csGRP78) has been validated as a functional receptor/co-receptor on tumor cells and certain stressed cells, and is a bona fide drug target for antibodies, ADCs, and other binders. It reaches the outer face by its own trafficking mechanism under these specific cell states.
